stories.title: Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war and lays out 5 conditions, state TV says
stories.description: Iran submitted a counterproposal that includes safeguards against future attacks against Iran and the country's right to "sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz," Iranian state Press TV reported.

stories.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

stories.title: The Israeli military wants several more weeks to fight Iran war, officials say
stories.description: The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.

stories.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
stories.description: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

stories.title: Trump declares victory and claims Iran offers a 'prize' in talks Iran has denied having
stories.description: President Trump said on Tuesday Iran made a valuable offer to reach a deal with the United States, in talks that Iran has denied are taking place. "We've won this. This war has been won," Trump said.

stories.title: Small window open for US-Iran talks, but swift end to war still unlikely
stories.description: President Trump says the US is already dealing with a "top person" in Iran, but Tehran denies that any talks have begun.

stories.title: What Iranians make of the possibility of talks to end the war
stories.description: Some Iranians dismiss the possibility of talks to end the war, others worry a deal may leave the current leaders in place.

stories.title: 'There's no safety anymore': Palestinians warn of expanding West Bank settler violence
stories.description: There has been a surge of attacks by Israeli settlers on Palestinian villages across the occupied territory since the start of the Iran war.

stories.title: Iran war shows norms of international conflicts have been overturned
stories.description: US threats against energy infrastructure, and Iran's retaliation on its Gulf neighbours, signal a clear change.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

stories.title: Iranian missiles injure 180 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
stories.description: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: 'It's been bittersweet': The Iranian-Iraqi cafe drawing crowds to Hackney
stories.description: Logma, a newly opened Iranian-Iraqi cafe in Hackney, has gone viral for its sandwiches.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Once seeking de-escalation, Saudi Arabia now sees ‘no coexistence’ with hardline Iran
stories.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Natasha Lindstaedt, Author, Professor of Government at the University of Essex. The unfolding conflict reveals a profound shift in Gulf strategic thinking, Professor Lindstaedt argues, driven less by ideological ambition than by accumulated inse...

stories.title: US goals shift: 'At the beginning of the Iran war, the US and Israel were on the same page'
stories.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Dr H. A. Hellyer, Senior Associate Fellow at the Royal United Services Institute (RUSI) for Defence and International Security Studies in London. He examines the evolving dynamics between Washington and Tel Aviv in the context of the war on Iran...

stories.title: US demands of 15-point peace plan likely 'unacceptable' for Iran
stories.description: The US announced it had sent on a 15-point peace plan to Iran, which Tehran has deemed 'excessive'. But what is the US's list of demands? There are some indications: sanction relief, a rollback of Iran's nuclear programme, the monitoring by the IAEA of that programme, shipping th...

stories.title: In Nairobi, residents blame flood devastation on inadequate infrastructure
stories.description: At least 21 Kenyan counties have been affected by floods since their start in March, raising concerns over public safety, infrastructure damage and growing humanitarian needs. According to authorities, over 34,000 people have been displaced. The capital, Nairobi, has been particu...

stories.title: Suspicious bets placed on US-Iran strikes trigger insider trading concerns
stories.description: Before financial markets opened in the United States on March 25, the US president stepped back from a threat to target Iranian energy sites. But 15 minutes before Trump's statement, thousands of oil contracts were traded - meaning anyone who placed bet on crude prices dropping w...

stories.title: Oil supply shock hardens energy transition resolve – for some
stories.description: The energy crisis over the Iran war has left some countries glad they've added more renewables, while others are doubling down on fossil fuels. But first: oil prices fall on hopes for de-escalation in the Middle East, while suspicions swirl over potential insider trading on Trump...

stories.title: Unclear 'how much of a negotiation is taking place' between US and Iran
stories.description: Iran has received a US plan to pause the war in the Middle East, officials said on March 25 — a proposal sent even as Washington deploys paratroopers and more Marines to the region. Tehran did not confirm receiving the plan and publicly dismissed Washington's diplomatic efforts. ...

stories.title: What do we know of the US's 15-point peace plan sent to Iran?
stories.description: Donald Trump says he has a 15-point plan that has been offered to Iran to bring an end to the war. But details remain vague and Trump's claims of progress in talks have been dismissed by Tehran. The US is hoping that talks hosted by Pakistan will be able to facilitate a settlemen...

stories.title: 'Mind-blowing corruption': Alleged insider trading over US-Israeli strikes in Iran
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, March 25: Papers discuss Donald Trump's 15-point plan and the US's "fog of diplomacy". Could it be staged for the global markets? Also: brick workers are coerced into selling their kidneys in Pakistan. Finally, Democrat Emily Gregory wins the hotly conte...

stories.title: Iran calls US proposal to end war ‘maximalist, unreasonable’
stories.description: This is a breaking news story.

stories.title: Missiles overhead, silence below: Israel’s home front holds firm
stories.description: Censorship and unity in the face of the Iranian threat combine to stifle dissent as Israel's war with Iran drags on.

stories.title: Hezbollah chief urges unity, no talks with Israel amid Lebanon attacks
stories.description: Naim Qassem says negotiating with Israel 'under fire amounts to imposing surrender' on Lebanon.

stories.title: Israel talks up Iranian threats to European cities to justify war
stories.description: Israel has been trying to make the case for its war on Iran by portraying the country as a threat to European cities.

stories.title: Iranian attacks amount to violation of sovereignty, Gulf states tell UN
stories.description: UN Human Rights Council passes resolution demanding end to Iran's 'unprovoked attacks' on countries in the Middle East.

stories.title: Trump’s war on Iran, in his own words
stories.description: Here’s a look at Trump’s war, in his own words.

stories.title: Asian Champions League quarterfinal sends Iran’s Tractor FC to Saudi Arabia
stories.description: Draw for AFC Asian Champions League Elite quarterfinals sees Tractor FC play Dubai's Shabab Al-Ahli in Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: US-Iran mediation: What are each side’s demands – and is a deal possible?
stories.description: Trump's envoys have sent a 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran via Pakistan. But Tehran has its own conditions.

stories.title: Large Polymarket, Wall Street bets on Trump’s war news under scrutiny
stories.description: Well-timed trades regarding Iran war news this week lead critics to argue White House may be engaged in insider trading.

stories.title: Spain’s PM says Israel to inflict on Lebanon same destruction as in Gaza
stories.description: Pedro ​Sanchez has been one of the few leaders in Europe to condemn Israel's war in Lebanon, US-Israeli ⁠war on Iran.

stories.title: UN human rights chief warns war on Iran is spreading
stories.description: UN human rights chief Volker Turk says the war on Iran is spreading across the region, with civilians bearing the brunt.

stories.title: Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz is an international crisis
stories.description: As such, it requires urgent action.

stories.title: ‘A heinous crime’: Air strikes kill seven fighters in Iraq’s Anbar
stories.description: Police source tells Al Jazeera attack hits positions of the Iran-aligned PMF, which the US has increasingly targeted.

stories.title: Iran rejects US proposal, lays out 5 conditions for ending war
stories.description: Iran’s state-owned broadcaster Press TV reported on Wednesday that Tehran had “responded negatively” to an American proposal aimed at ending the US-Israeli war on the country. The report cited a senior political-security official. “The official with knowledge of the details of th...

stories.title: Pakistan has conveyed US proposal; Turkiye or Pakistan could host talks, senior Iranian official says
stories.description: Pakistan delivered a proposal from the United States to Iran, and either Pakistan or Turkiye could be venues for discussions to de-escalate the war in the Gulf, a senior Iranian official told Reuters on Wednesday. The comments, by an official speaking on condition of anonymity, w...

stories.title: Iranian authorities reject Trump’s statements about negotiating with Tehran
stories.description: Head of Iran's government information council Elias Hazrati stated that the US had failed to achieve anything, failed to give answers to the global public

stories.title: Russian LNG supplies may be temporarily exempt from sanctions — investor
stories.description: Kyle Shostak assumed that natural gas, especially for European buyers, will be in the price range of $3.30-3.80 per MMBtu

stories.title: Iran claims US spy plane sustained $2.5-million-damage — agency
stories.description: No information was provided regarding the circumstances of the reported incident

stories.title: Turkey trying to persuade Arab countries not to interfere in Iran conflict — Bloomberg
stories.description: According to the agency, Ankara fears that the involvement of Arab states in the Middle East in the hostilities could lead to a full-scale war

stories.title: Turkey, Pakistan seen as possible venues for US-Iran negotiations — agency
stories.description: According to the source, Pakistan has sent to Iran a proposal from the United States to resolve the conflict

stories.title: IAEA head believes nuclear agreement with Iran is possible
stories.description: It’s important for Iran to protect the principle of nuclear autonomy, Rafael Grossi noted

stories.title: Only Iran has right to decide what to do with its nuclear materials — Russian diplomat
stories.description: Maria Zakharova stressed that Iran’s legitimate right under the NPT could not be called into question

stories.title: Europe will have to turn back to Russian for energy — Orban
stories.description: The head of the Hungarian government noted that EU leaders created the threat of an energy crisis by first imposing sanctions against Russian companies and then deciding to cut off oil and gas supplies from Russia

stories.title: Russia will continue cooperation with Iran — Energy Minister
stories.description: Sergey Tsivilyov emphasized that he believed in the prospects for cooperation

stories.title: Calls for talks on Iran could be smokescreen for regrouping of forces — Russian MFA
stories.description: The very fact that diplomatic talks had been used to cover up preparations for war overtly discredits diplomacy, Maria Zakharova pointed out

stories.title: Conflict over Iran could lead to rise of ‘petroyuan’ — Deutsche Bank report
stories.description: The report notes that the American "security umbrella" has faced serious challenges amid attacks on American bases, oil fields, and infrastructure in the Middle East

stories.title: Somali pirates reportedly seize Iranian ship in Indian Ocean — maritime security center
stories.description: According to the report, the incident took place some 400 nautical miles east of Mogadishu

stories.title: Escalation of conflict with Iran could be very costly for US — expert
stories.description: Daniel Davis, retired US Army Lieutenant Colonel, also noted that the United States desperately needs negotiations

stories.title: Majority of Americans believe operation against Iran has gone too far — poll
stories.description: About two-thirds of US citizens think that preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons should be a top priority for US foreign policy

stories.title: Israeli army claims to have hit Iran’s only submarine development facility
stories.description: Various models of unmanned boats were also manufactured at this facility

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
